stories.hook: Sheep show the root network before the payoff.
stories.lead: Sheep grazing keeps land alive. Watch this one twice. See how they only bite tops of grass. That’s why fields stay green and wildflowers bloom. Because they leave roots intact, soil breathes, and insects have homes. Tiny hooves actually stir up seeds too. You ever notice how quiet they get when grazing. That’s focus—eating only what they need.
